calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court received an email earlier at 9:11 am contesting the tentative ruling, but no one showed up. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER ALEX HISEN's Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings is Off calendar. The parties did not file any supplemental papers and the court assumes that they have resolved the details regarding how the arbitration will proceed.

Judge: Newton Lam, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR# 6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/NL)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-13-2017 Continued To Dec-21-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jack W. Weaver of the law offices of Welty & Welty PC (707-433-4842) appears as counsel for petitioner/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER ALEX HISEN's Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings is CONTINUED to December 21, 2017 at 2:00 PM in Department 302/Law and Motion to be heard by Judge Newton Lam.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jack Weaver, Esq. of Welty Welty PC, Tel. 707.433.4482, for petitioner and moving party;
Michael Aparicio, Esq. of Davis Wright, Tel. 415.276.4892, for respondent.

DISCLOSURE: In the course of argument, Judge Lam disclosed that he is acquainted Judge Ware. He does not believe that this can affect his ability to be fair and impartial in this case. There was no objection by either party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER ALEX HISEN's Motion For Complete Consolidation Of Separate Arbitration Proceedings is GRANTED and the hearing is CONTINUED to December 13, 2017 to allow petitioner Hisen and respondent SysIQ, Inc time to come to an agreement as to before which tribunal and under what terms the consolidated action will proceed and if the parties fail to reach an agreement the court will resolve the inconsistency between the arbitration provisions and will select the tribunal in the interests of justice. California has a strong policy favoring consolidating arbitrations involving common issues of law and fact. Gordon v. G.R.O.U.P., Inc. (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 998, 1006. The statutory elements of section Code Civ. Proc., 1281.3 are met. Even assuming MetaLinQ LLC and MetaLinQ are third parties the proceedings need not be between exactly the same parties but also provide for the situation where "one party is a party to a separate arbitration agreement or proceeding with a third party." The disputes arise from the same transactions or series of related transactions because the sale of the assets under the Asset Agreement from which the claims about breach of contract and fraud arise is a transaction related to the hiring and employment of Mr. Hissen under the Employment Agreement, under which Mr. Hissen's claim for severance arises. Moreover it appears from SysIQ, Inc's AAA claim that Mr. Hisen's performance as an employee had relevance to his obligations under the Asset Acquisition agreement. The disputes have common issues of fact and law, because factual allegations in SysIQ, Inc's action under the Asset Agreement assert that Mr. Hissen "actively sought to interfere with SysIQ's efforts to completely transfer assets" and concealed the inferior quality of the assets by "refusing to work with Claimant's existing software experts and coders, already employed, and instead hiring only certain individuals who Hisen hand selected" (AAA Claim 11,13). SysIQ, Inc also alleges that Mr.Hissen "has not met the basic terms of the Employment Agreement" and gives examples of breaches (AAA Complaint at 15.) There is a possibility that a finding of a breach of the Asset Acquisition Agreement would have a preclusive effect on the employment claims under the JAMS agreement or lead to conflicting ruling. There is also the possibility of conflicting financings with respect to the common issues of fact. Respondent SysIQ, Inc has not shown that Mr. Hisen waived the right to move for a consolidation of the proceedings. Mr. Hisen has been raising objections to the concurrent arbitrations from the commencement of the AAA proceedings both before the AAA and JAMS and SysIQ and under all the relevant circumstances Mr. Hisen's conduct with respect to the AAA proceedings does not imply that P waived his right to seek judicial consolidation.

Counsel for plaintiff is to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org.
